nodes.title: Validate the ordinary constructor contract first
nodes.why: The scoped helper requires a callable right-hand side whose prototype is an object or function, and invalid inputs must throw instead of silently returning false.
nodes.alternative: Read the prototype only after checking the left value, which would skip this helper's up-front RHS validation contract.
nodes.tradeoff: Predictable ordinary-constructor validation, while custom Symbol.hasInstance and bound-function internals remain out of scope.

nodes.title: Return false for null and primitive left values

anchor.snippet: if (obj === null || obj === undefined) return false;

nodes.why: Only objects and functions have a prototype chain, so valid primitive left values are false without boxing.
nodes.alternative: Call Object.getPrototypeOf on every left value, which would coerce or fail instead of expressing the contract.
nodes.tradeoff: Clear left-value behavior after the right-hand-side contract has been validated.

nodes.title: Walk upward until match or chain end
nodes.why: This models the ordinary prototype-chain core: compare each prototype link by reference.
nodes.tradeoff: Great for learning internals, but it does not cover custom `Symbol.hasInstance` behavior.
